basics.name: Minghao Ning
basics.label: PhD student, University of Waterloo
basics.summary: PhD student in Mechanical and Mechatronics Engineering at the University of Waterloo, working on autonomous driving, multi-sensor fusion, motion planning, control, cooperative perception, and trustworthy mobility systems.

work.summary: Research in autonomous driving perception, planning, control, safety, and cooperative infrastructure sensing.
highlights: Developed point-cloud-based potential-field motion planning methods for autonomous vehicles.
Designed LiDAR-camera-HD map fusion approaches for safe drivable space generation.
Built cooperative perception systems using connected infrastructure sensor nodes.

education.area: MASc / PhD in Mechanical and Mechatronics Engineering
Bachelor of Engineering in Vehicle Engineering

publications.name: CoInfra: A Large-Scale Cooperative Infrastructure Perception System and Dataset in Adverse Weather
publications.summary: Large-scale cooperative infrastructure perception system and dataset for adverse-weather vehicle-infrastructure cooperation.

publications.name: A Novel Motion Planning for Autonomous Vehicles Using Point Cloud Based Potential Field
publications.summary: Point-cloud-based potential-field motion planning for autonomous vehicles.

publications.name: An Efficient Approach to Generate Safe Drivable Space by LiDAR-Camera-HDmap Fusion
publications.summary: LiDAR-camera-HD map fusion for safe drivable space generation.

publications.name: Enhancing Indoor Mobility with Connected Sensor Nodes: A Real-Time, Delay-Aware Cooperative Perception Approach
publications.summary: Delay-aware cooperative perception for intelligent indoor mobility.

presentations.name: Trustworthy Diagnosis in Connected Mobility
presentations.description: Organized and chaired a technical session on trustworthy diagnosis and prognosis in connected and cooperative automated mobility.
